title: CD Genomics Tutorial
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7mvnt","text":"Learn how to submit samples to CD Genomics for bioinformatic processing ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: CD Genomics is a leader in high-throughput sequencing services. Their MicrobioSeq platform is specifically designed for metagenomic research.
CD Genomics has ability to handle diverse sample types—from soil and water to clinical or industrial samples—and deliver high-quality data at competitive prices.
16S/18S/ITS Amplicon Sequencing: Starts at around $150 per sample , with discounts for larger batches.
Shotgun Metagenomics: Starts at approximately $300 per sample . This is ideal for whole-genome analysis.
Customization options like deeper sequencing or specific bioinformatics pipelines may increase the cost, so it’s best to request a quote for precise pricing.
Before sending your samples, there are a few things you need to prepare:
Sample Type:
CD Genomics accepts DNA extracts or raw biological materials. Check their guidelines for your specific sample type.
DNA Quality and Quantity:
For DNA extracts, ensure the concentration is ≥50 ng/μL, with a total amount of at least 500 ng.
Use high-purity methods to minimize contaminants that could interfere with sequencing.
Sample Storage and Shipping:
Freeze samples immediately after preparation.
Use dry ice for shipping to maintain integrity during transit.
Step One:
Visit their website at cd-genomics.com and go to the MicrobioSeq section.
Fill out the online submission form with details about your samples and project requirements.
Step Two:
Once you’ve completed the form, you’ll receive a confirmation email with instructions for labeling and shipping your samples.
Step Three:
Pack your samples securely with dry ice or gel packs, depending on the material.
Step Four:
Ship your samples to the address provided in the email. Ensure you include any required documentation, like a printed copy of your submission form.
Data Turnaround: Most projects are completed within 2–3 weeks, depending on complexity.

Results: You’ll receive high-quality raw data and a bioinformatics report, including taxonomic profiling, functional analysis, or custom analyses tailored to your needs. Support: Need help interpreting your data? CD Genomics offers post-project support to ensure you get the most out of your results."
creator: Paul McLean
